# Issue #8764: Vectorize DiceHelper.__call__()
**Repository:** Project-MONAI/MONAI
**Author:** aymuos15
**Status:** open
**Created At:** 2026-03-03T17:31:57Z

## Description
## Summary
- Replace nested batch/channel Python loops in `DiceHelper.__call__()` with vectorized torch operations
- Remove dead `compute_channel` method (no longer called after vectorization)

## Note on memory
The current implementation vectorizes across both batch and channel dimensions simultaneously, which increases peak memory for large 3D volumes. If this becomes an issue, we can switch to looping over batch while keeping channels vectorized — this retains most of the speedup while keeping memory proportional to a single sample.

## Test plan
- [x] All existing `test_compute_meandice` tests pass
- [x] New test cases added covering mixed-format edge cases:
  - Single-channel `y` (class indices) + multi-channel `y_pred` (one-hot)
  - Single-channel `y_pred` (argmaxed, with `num_classes`) + multi-channel `y` (one-hot)
  - Same as above with `include_background=False` and batched inputs

## Comments
### Comment by coderabbitai[bot] at 2026-03-03T17:32:51Z
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: summarize by coderabbit.ai -->
<!-- walkthrough_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

## Walkthrough

The PR refactors DiceHelper in monai/metrics/meandice.py by removing the per-channel `compute_channel` method and implementing a vectorized Dice computation inside `DiceHelper.__call__`. Predictions and targets are converted to boolean masks (supporting multi-channel one-hot and single-channel class indices), spatial dims are flattened, and per-(batch,channel) intersections and sums are computed in a single vectorized operation. Background inclusion/exclusion and `ignore_empty` semantics (producing NaNs or per-case values) are preserved. Tests are extended with three mixed-format cases exercising one-hot/argmax predictions and class-index targets.

## Estimated code review effort

🎯 3 (Moderate) | ⏱️ ~25 minutes

</details>

<!-- walkthrough_end -->
